Task lifecycle contract: When Slack asks to capture Thermal product or engineering work, create the task in Product Build list `901714674310` with initial status `to do`; a freshly captured task has neither `prepped` nor `agent-ready`. Prep (ignite-prep) may add `agent-ready` only after the description contains a canonical `## ⚙️ Execution Brief` ending exactly `Execution-ready: YES`, every product decision is resolved, all native predecessor tasks are complete, and the task has exactly one colon-form model floor (`model:haiku`, `model:sonnet`, `model:opus`, or `model:fable`). Executor (ignite-execute) claims ready work and ships it to `in review`; Validator (ignite-validate) is the only actor that moves it to `complete` and leaves an `ignite-validate:` proof comment.
